The classification of Ochradiscus places it within the kingdom Protista, phylum Ochrophyta, and class Bacillariophyceae. The genus Ochradiscus is further divided into several species, each with unique characteristics. The classification of Ochradiscus is essential for understanding its ecological role and evolutionary history.

intro: Ochradiscus, also known as Ochradiscus tutelus, is a type of green algae commonly found in freshwater environments. The health benefits of Ochradiscus include its potential to stimulate the immune system and exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and improving overall health. The bioactive constituents of Ochradiscus include various polysaccharides, such as fucoidan and laminarin, as well as other compounds like carotenoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to its bioactive properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ochradiscus is often prepared into infusions, decoctions, or capsules, and is used to support immune function and reduce oxidative stress.
